message:
test: standardize assert function signatures (message-first pattern)

BREAKING CHANGE TO TEST FRAMEWORK API:

The assert_exit_code, assert_success, and assert_fails functions now follow
a consistent "message-first" argument pattern:

  BEFORE: assert_exit_code 0 command arg1 arg2 "description message"
  AFTER:  assert_exit_code 0 "description message" command arg1 arg2

Rationale for this change:
- The previous pattern placed the message as the last argument, which
  required complex array manipulation (extracting the last element via
  ${args[-1]} and unsetting it) to separate the command from the message
- This was fragile when commands had variable numbers of arguments
- The new pattern is simpler: fixed positional args (code, message) first,
  then the command with all its arguments via "$@"
- This matches the pattern used by assert_equals, assert_contains, etc.

Implementation changes (test_framework.sh):
- assert_exit_code: Takes expected_code, message, then command
- assert_success: Takes message, then command
- assert_fails: Takes message, then command
- Removed complex array slicing logic in favor of simple shift operations

Updated test files to use new signature:
- test_framework_selftest.sh (3 functions updated)
- test_unit_gh_actions.sh (6 assert calls updated)
- test_unit_non_interactive.sh (4 assert calls updated)
- test_unit_review_push.sh (2 assert calls updated)
- test_unit_worktree.sh (4 assert calls updated)

All tests must be updated atomically to avoid intermediate failures.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-705","title":"Implement sanitize_path_segment()","description":"**Sanitize path components**

## What
Remove or replace unsafe characters from path segments.

## Why
Malicious or unusual repo names could cause filesystem issues. Defense in depth.

## Implementation
```bash
sanitize_path_segment() {
    local segment="$1"
    # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
    segment="${segment#"${segment%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    segment="${segment%"${segment##*[![:space:]]}"}"
    # Replace potentially problematic characters
    segment="${segment//\//_}"
    segment="${segment//\\/_}"
    segment="${segment//:/_}"
    echo "$segment"
}
```

## Characters to Handle
- Slashes (/ \)
- Colons (problematic on Windows)
- Leading dots (hidden files)
- Control characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dangerous characters replaced
- Empty segments rejected
- Normal names pass through unchanged","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sanitize_path_segment() with comprehensive character replacement and safety checks","labels":["parsing","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-705","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.707274655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

Parsing is critical. Bugs here cause repos to be cloned to wrong locations or fail entirely.

 {"id":"bd-73ys","title":"Implement discovery summary display and formatting","description":"# Task: Implement Discovery Summary Display

## Purpose
Format and display discovered work items in a user-friendly summary, especially for --dry-run mode. This gives users visibility into what will be reviewed before sessions start.

## Background
After GraphQL discovery and priority scoring, users need to see:
- Total work items found across all repos
- Breakdown by priority level (CRITICAL/HIGH/NORMAL/LOW)
- Breakdown by type (issues vs PRs)
- Top N items that will be processed
- Repos with most activity

## Implementation

### show_discovery_summary()
```bash
show_discovery_summary() {
    local -n items_ref=$1
    local max_repos="${2:-$REVIEW_PARALLEL}"
    
    # Count totals
    local total=${#items_ref[@]}
    local critical=0 high=0 normal=0 low=0
    local issues=0 prs=0
    
    for item in "${items_ref[@]}"; do
        IFS="|" read -r repo type number title score level _ _ <<< "$item"
        case "$level" in
            CRITICAL) ((critical++)) ;;
            HIGH) ((high++)) ;;
            NORMAL) ((normal++)) ;;
            LOW) ((low++)) ;;
        esac
        case "$type" in
            issue) ((issues++)) ;;
            pr) ((prs++)) ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Display summary
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        show_discovery_summary_gum
    else
        show_discovery_summary_ansi
    fi
}
```

### show_discovery_summary_gum()
```bash
show_discovery_summary_gum() {
    gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" --border-foreground "#fab387" \
        "$(gum style --bold "Discovery Summary")"
    
    echo ""
    gum style "Total work items: $total"
    gum style "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs"
    echo ""
    
    # Priority breakdown with colors
    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" "  CRITICAL: $critical"
    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#fab387" "  HIGH: $high"
    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "  NORMAL: $normal"
    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "  LOW: $low"
    echo ""
    
    # Top items preview
    gum style --bold "Top $max_repos items to review:"
    local i=0
    for item in "${items_ref[@]:0:$max_repos}"; do
        ((i++))
        IFS="|" read -r repo type number title score level _ _ <<< "$item"
        local badge
        badge=$(format_priority_badge "$level")
        echo "  $i. $badge $repo#$number: ${title:0:50}"
    done
}
```

### show_discovery_summary_ansi()
ANSI fallback version using escape codes:
```bash
show_discovery_summary_ansi() {
    local BOLD="\033[1m"
    local RED="\033[31m"
    local ORANGE="\033[33m"
    local YELLOW="\033[93m"
    local GRAY="\033[90m"
    local RESET="\033[0m"
    
    echo -e "${BOLD}Discovery Summary${RESET}"
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Total work items: $total"
    echo "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs"
    echo ""
    echo "By priority:"
    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${RED}CRITICAL: $critical${RESET}"
    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${ORANGE}HIGH: $high${RESET}"
    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${YELLOW}NORMAL: $normal${RESET}"
    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${GRAY}LOW: $low${RESET}"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Top items to review:${RESET}"
    # ... similar item listing
}
```

### format_priority_badge()
```bash
format_priority_badge() {
    local level="$1"
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        case "$level" in
            CRITICAL) gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" --bold "CRITICAL" ;;
            HIGH) gum style --foreground "#fab387" "HIGH" ;;
            NORMAL) gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "NORMAL" ;;
            LOW) gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "LOW" ;;
        esac
    else
        echo "[$level]"
    fi
}
```

### JSON Output Mode
```bash
show_discovery_summary_json() {
    jq -n \
        --argjson total "$total" \
        --argjson issues "$issues" \
        --argjson prs "$prs" \
        --argjson critical "$critical" \
        --argjson high "$high" \
        --argjson normal "$normal" \
        --argjson low "$low" \
        --argjson items "$(printf "%s\n" "${items_ref[@]}" | head -20 | jq -R -s "split(\"\n\") | map(select(. \!= \"\"))")" \
        "{
            total: \$total,
            by_type: {issues: \$issues, prs: \$prs},
            by_priority: {critical: \$critical, high: \$high, normal: \$normal, low: \$low},
            top_items: \$items
        }"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify gum formatting renders correctly
- Verify ANSI fallback works without gum
- Verify JSON output is valid
- Verify priority colors are correct
- Test with 0 items, 1 item, many items

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary displays item counts correctly
- [ ] Priority breakdown shows correct colors
- [ ] Top items listed with truncated titles
- [ ] Works with both gum and ANSI fallback
- [ ] JSON output mode for automation

-{"id":"bd-7itw","title":"Add review prerequisites to ru doctor command","description":"# Task: Add Review Prerequisites to Doctor

## Purpose
Extend `ru doctor` to check prerequisites for the review command, helping users diagnose why `ru review` might fail.

## Background
From AGENTS.md:
- doctor command does system diagnostics
- Should check for all dependencies

## New Doctor Checks for Review

### 1. Claude Code Installation
```bash
check_claude_code() {
    if \! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Claude Code not installed"
        log_info "  Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check version for stream-json support
    local version
    version=$(claude --version 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    log_success "Claude Code installed: $version"
    return 0
}
```

### 2. Stream-JSON Support
```bash
check_stream_json_support() {
    # Test that stream-json format works
    if claude -p "test" --output-format stream-json --help 2>&1 | grep -q "unknown"; then
        log_error "Claude Code version does not support --output-format stream-json"
        log_info "  Update: npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "Stream-JSON output format supported"
    return 0
}
```

### 3. tmux Installation (for local driver)
```bash
check_tmux() {
    if \! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "tmux not installed (required for local review driver)"
        log_info "  Install: brew install tmux  OR  apt install tmux"
        return 1
    fi
    
    local version
    version=$(tmux -V 2>/dev/null)
    log_success "tmux installed: $version"
    return 0
}
```

### 4. ntm Installation (optional, for advanced driver)
```bash
check_ntm() {
    if \! command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "ntm not installed (optional, for advanced review mode)"
        log_info "  See: https://github.com/dicklesworthstone/ntm"
        return 0  # Not an error, just informational
    fi
    
    # Check robot mode support
    if \! ntm --help 2>&1 | grep -q "robot"; then
        log_warn "ntm installed but robot mode not available"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "ntm installed with robot mode support"
    return 0
}
```

### 5. jq Installation
```bash
check_jq() {
    if \! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        log_error "jq not installed (required for review command)"
        log_info "  Install: brew install jq  OR  apt install jq"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "jq installed"
    return 0
}
```

### 6. GitHub API Rate Limit
```bash
check_github_rate_limit() {
    local remaining reset
    remaining=$(gh api rate_limit --jq ".resources.core.remaining" 2>/dev/null)
    reset=$(gh api rate_limit --jq ".resources.core.reset" 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ -z "$remaining" ]]; then
        log_warn "Could not check GitHub rate limit"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [[ $remaining -lt 100 ]]; then
        local reset_time
        reset_time=$(date -d "@$reset" 2>/dev/null || date -r "$reset" 2>/dev/null)
        log_warn "GitHub API rate limit low: $remaining remaining"
        log_info "  Resets at: $reset_time"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "GitHub API rate limit OK: $remaining remaining"
    return 0
}
```

### 7. Review State Directory
```bash
check_review_state_dir() {
    local state_dir="${RU_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.local/state/ru}"
    
    if [[ \! -d "$state_dir" ]]; then
        log_info "Review state directory will be created on first run"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if [[ \! -w "$state_dir" ]]; then
        log_error "Review state directory not writable: $state_dir"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "Review state directory OK: $state_dir"
    return 0
}
```

## Integration

### Doctor Output Section
```
Review Prerequisites:
  ✓ Claude Code installed: 1.0.0
  ✓ Stream-JSON output format supported
  ✓ tmux installed: tmux 3.3a
  ℹ ntm not installed (optional)
  ✓ jq installed
  ✓ GitHub API rate limit OK: 4850 remaining
  ✓ Review state directory OK
```

### Conditional Checks
Only run review checks if:
- User runs `ru doctor --review`
- User has ever run `ru review` before
- There is a review config file

## Testing
- Verify each check works independently
- Verify missing dependencies detected
- Verify output formatting correct
- Verify --review flag works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claude Code check works
- [ ] Stream-JSON support check works
- [ ] tmux/ntm checks work
- [ ] jq check works
- [ ] Rate limit check works
- [ ] State directory check works
- [ ] Clean doctor output format

 {"id":"bd-807","title":"Implement do_pull()","description":"**Pull with strategy support**

## What
Function to pull updates with configurable strategy (ff-only, rebase, merge).

## Why
Different workflows need different pull strategies. ff-only is safe default, but some users prefer rebase.

## Implementation
```bash
do_pull() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local repo_name="$2"
    local strategy="${3:-ff-only}"
    local autostash="${4:-false}"

    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would pull: $repo_name (strategy: $strategy)"
        write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "dry_run" "" ""
        return 0
    fi

    local start_time
    start_time=$(date +%s)

    # Build pull args
    local pull_args=()
    case "$strategy" in
        ff-only) pull_args+=(--ff-only) ;;
        rebase)  pull_args+=(--rebase) ;;
        merge)   pull_args+=(--no-ff) ;;
    esac

    [[ "$autostash" == "true" ]] && pull_args+=(--autostash)

    # Get current HEAD for comparison
    local old_head
    old_head=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    local output
    if output=$(git -C "$repo_path" pull "${pull_args[@]}" 2>&1); then
        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
        local new_head
        new_head=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [[ "$old_head" == "$new_head" ]]; then
            log_info "Already current: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "current" "$duration" ""
        else
            log_success "Pulled: $repo_name (${duration}s)"
            write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "updated" "$duration" ""
        fi
        return 0
    else
        local exit_code=$?
        local reason="failed"

        if [[ "$output" =~ (divergent|cannot\ be\ fast-forwarded) ]]; then
            reason="diverged"
            log_warn "Diverged: $repo_name (needs manual merge or --rebase)"
        elif [[ "$output" =~ (conflict|CONFLICT) ]]; then
            reason="conflict"
            log_error "Merge conflict: $repo_name"
        else
            log_error "Pull failed: $repo_name"
        fi

        write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "$reason" "" "$output"
        return $exit_code
    fi
}
```

 {"id":"bd-809","title":"Handle branch not found gracefully","description":"**Graceful handling when repo@branch branch doesn't exist**

## What
When user specifies repo@branch and branch doesn't exist, handle gracefully.

## Why
Branches get deleted, renamed, or typos happen. Current plan doesn't address this.

## Behavior
```
$ ru sync owner/repo@nonexistent
Cloning: owner/repo
  Warning: Branch 'nonexistent' not found
  Falling back to default branch (main)
```

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    # ... clone repo ...
    
    if [[ -n "$branch" ]]; then
        if git -C "$target_dir" rev-parse --verify "origin/$branch" &>/dev/null; then
            git -C "$target_dir" checkout "$branch" --quiet
        else
            log_warn "Branch '$branch' not found, using default branch"
            write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "branch_not_found" "" "Branch: $branch"
        fi
    fi
}
```

 {"id":"bd-810","title":"Handle network timeouts gracefully","description":"**Handle network timeouts gracefully**

## What
Add timeout handling for clone and pull operations.

## Why
Flaky networks, slow connections, or overloaded servers can cause operations to hang indefinitely. Users need a way to bound wait time.

## Implementation
- Set GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT=1000 and GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME=30 as defaults
- These cause git to abort if transfer rate drops below 1KB/s for 30 seconds
- Add --timeout N option to override (sets GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME)
- On timeout, log error and continue to next repo
- Report timeouts distinctly in summary (not just 'failed')

 {"id":"bd-ff8h","title":"Implement GraphQL batched repository discovery","description":"# Task: Implement GraphQL Batched Repository Discovery

## Purpose
Efficiently discover open issues and PRs across all configured repos using GraphQL alias batching, reducing API calls from O(n) to O(n/25).

## Background: Why GraphQL Batching?
- REST API: 1 call per repo for issues + 1 for PRs = 2n calls
- GraphQL aliases: Query 25 repos in single call = n/25 calls
- For 100 repos: 200 REST calls → 4 GraphQL calls (50× reduction)
- Avoids rate limiting, faster discovery

## Implementation Details

### gh_api_graphql_repo_batch()
```bash
gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() {
    local chunk="$1"
    local q="query {"
    local i=0

    while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
        local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
        local name="${repo_id#*/}"

        q+=" repo${i}: repository(owner:\"${owner}\", name:\"${name}\") {"
        q+=" nameWithOwner isArchived isFork updatedAt"
        # Issues with metadata for scoring
        q+=" issues(states:OPEN, first:50, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # PRs with metadata for scoring
        q+=" pullRequests(states:OPEN, first:20, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt isDraft"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # Oldest open issue for staleness
        q+=" oldestIssue: issues(states:OPEN, first:1, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:ASC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { createdAt }"
        q+=" }"
        q+=" }"
        ((i++))
    done <<< "$chunk"

    q+=" }"
    
    gh api graphql -f query="$q" 2>/dev/null
}
```

### parse_graphql_work_items()
Uses jq to flatten response into TSV for easy Bash parsing:
```bash
parse_graphql_work_items() {
    local resp="$1"

    echo "$resp" | jq -r '
        .data | to_entries[] | select(.value != null) |
        select(.value.isArchived != true) |
        select(.value.isFork != true) |
        .value as $repo |
        (
            # Issues
            ($repo.issues.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "issue", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, "false"] | @tsv
        ),
        (
            # PRs
            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, (.isDraft | tostring)] | @tsv
        )
    ' 2>/dev/null
}
```

### chunk_repo_ids()
Split repo list into chunks of 25:
```bash
chunk_repo_ids() {
    local chunk_size="$1"
    shift
    local repos=("$@")
    local count=0
    local chunk=""
    
    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
        chunk+="$repo"$'\n'
        ((count++))
        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
            echo "$chunk"
            chunk=""
            count=0
        fi
    done
    
    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && echo "$chunk"
}
```

### discover_work_items()
Main discovery function that:
1. Gets all repos from config
2. Resolves to owner/repo format
3. Filters non-GitHub hosts
4. Chunks into batches of 25
5. Executes GraphQL queries
6. Parses responses into work items
7. Calculates priority scores
8. Sorts by score descending
9. Applies max_repos limit if specified

## Data Format
Work item string format (pipe-separated):
```
repo_id|type|number|title|score|level|created_at|updated_at
owner/repo|issue|42|Authentication failing|85|HIGH|2024-12-15T...|2025-01-01T...
```

## Error Handling
- Skip repos that fail resolution
- Continue on individual query failures
- Log warnings for rate limit responses
- Graceful degradation if GraphQL unavailable

## Dependencies
- jq for JSON parsing (already assumed by ru)
- gh CLI with GraphQL support

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL response with fixture
- Verify chunking works correctly
- Verify archived/forked repos filtered
- Verify TSV parsing handles edge cases
- Verify 25-repo chunks respected

- [ ] Issues and PRs both discovered

- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded

 {"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:18:06.99800939-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:18:06.99800939-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented rate-limit governor with: GOVERNOR_STATE tracking, GitHub/model rate limit detection, circuit breaker pattern, adaptive parallelism. 13 unit tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:17:00.313011296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver
- Common code calls driver_* functions
- Drivers can extend interface with driver-specific features
- Capabilities check enables graceful feature degradation

## Testing
- Mock driver for unit tests
- Verify interface consistency between drivers
- Test driver selection logic
- Verify event normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interface defined with all required operations
- [ ] Driver selection works correctly
- [ ] Event schema documented and consistent
- [ ] Capabilities query implemented
- [ ] Driver loading mechanism works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.284949263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","close_reason":"Unified driver interface implemented with: load_review_driver(), driver_capabilities(), driver_start_session(), driver_send_to_session(), driver_get_session_state(), driver_stop_session(), driver_interrupt_session(), driver_stream_events(), driver_list_sessions(), driver_session_alive().  {"id":"bd-mnu9","title":"Add cmd_review() command skeleton with argument parsing","description":"# Task: Add cmd_review() Command Skeleton

## Purpose
Create the entry point for all review functionality - the cmd_review() function that will orchestrate the entire review workflow.

## Implementation Details

### Location in ru script
Add after cmd_prune() (around line 3634), following existing command pattern.

### Function Structure
```bash
cmd_review() {
    local mode="auto"
    local parallel=4
    local dry_run="false"
    local resume="false"
    local apply="false"
    local push="false"
    local priority_threshold="all"
    local max_repos=""
    local max_runtime=""
    local max_questions=""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_review_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_review_prerequisites || exit 3

    # Generate unique run ID
    REVIEW_RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"

    # Acquire global lock
    acquire_review_lock || { log_error "Another review is running"; exit 1; }

    # Auto-detect driver
    [[ "$mode" == "auto" ]] && mode=$(detect_review_driver)

    # Discovery phase
    log_step "Scanning repositories for open issues and PRs..."
    local -a work_items
    discover_work_items work_items "$priority_threshold" "$max_repos"

    if [[ ${#work_items[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No work items need review"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # Show summary
    show_discovery_summary "${work_items[@]}"

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Dry run - exiting without starting sessions"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # ... orchestration phases follow
}
```

+test_execute_gh_actions_runs_commands() {
+    local test_name="execute_gh_actions: runs commands from plan"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+
+    export GH_LOG="$env_root/gh.log"
+    : > "$GH_LOG"
+
+    local bin_dir="$env_root/bin"
+    _write_mock_gh "$bin_dir" "ok"
+
+    local old_path="$PATH"
+    export PATH="$bin_dir:$PATH"
+
+    local plan_file="$env_root/plan.json"
+    create_valid_plan_with_actions "$plan_file"
+
+    # Run actions
+    assert_exit_code 0 "execute_gh_actions should succeed" execute_gh_actions "owner/repo" "$plan_file"
+
+    # Verify log file
+    local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review/gh-actions.jsonl"
+    assert_file_exists "$log_file" "Actions log should be created"
+
+    # Check for success entries
+    if grep -q '"status":"ok"' "$log_file"; then
+        pass "Log contains success status"
+    else
+        fail "Log missing success status"
+    fi
+
+    # Verify idempotence
+    assert_exit_code 0 "second run should still succeed" execute_gh_actions "owner/repo" "$plan_file"
+    if grep -q '"status":"skipped"' "$log_file"; then
+        pass "Log contains skipped status on second run"
+    else
+        fail "Log missing skipped status"
+    fi
+
+    export PATH="$old_path"
+    cleanup_temp_dirs
+}
+

+

+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+
+    local plan_file="$env_root/plan.json"
+    create_valid_plan_with_actions "$plan_file"
+
+    # Mock gh failure

+    export -f gh
+
+    assert_exit_code 1 "should return non-zero when any action fails" execute_gh_actions "owner/repo" "$plan_file"
+

+}
